Title: Flavonoid Biosynthesis. A Colorful Model for Genetics, Biochemistry, Cell Biology, and Biotechnology

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MATE (multidrug and toxic compound extrusion) family transporter
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for transport of proanthocyanidin precursors into the vacuole in seed coat endothelium.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning and characterization of tt12 mutant
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT12 encodes a MATE-family secondary transporter. 2. TT12 is required for vacuolar import of proanthocyanidin precursors in the Arabidopsis seed coat endothelium. 3. TT12 defines a transport step essential for flavonoid deposition rather than catalytic synthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a transport component for flavonoid sequestration, distinct from GST/ABC-dependent vacuolar pigment transport described in maize and petunia.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed coat flavonoid deposition and pigmentation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering flavonoid sequestration and seed coat proanthocyanidin accumulation by manipulating vacuolar transport capacity.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for vacuolar sequestration of flavonoid pigments.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant complementation and transport-related functional studies cited in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AN9 is a GST required for vacuolar sequestration of flavonoids in petunia flowers. 2. AN9 can complement flavonoid sequestration deficiencies across species. 3. It may function as an escort protein rather than by catalyzing glutathione conjugation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Widely divergent from maize BZ2 yet functionally analogous; proposed escort-protein role.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be co-engineered with biosynthetic genes to ensure efficient pigment sequestration in ornamental crops.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for vacuolar sequestration of flavonoid pigments in maize aleurone.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant and functional transport studies cited in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BZ2 is a GST necessary for vacuolar sequestration of flavonoids in maize. 2. BZ2 is functionally analogous to petunia AN9. 3. The GST may act as an escort protein rather than a classical conjugating enzyme.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts with an ABC-type glutathione pump in pigment transport.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant as a supporting transport factor when engineering anthocyanin deposition in cereal tissues.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: May catalyze an early reductive step in condensed tannin biosynthesis as a leucoanthocyanidin reductase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for condensed tannin biosynthesis in Arabidopsis seed coat.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BANYULS encodes a DFR-like protein associated with the ban mutant phenotype. 2. BAN likely acts as an LCR in an early step of condensed tannin biosynthesis. 3. BAN offers a more branch-specific engineering target than modulating DFR alone.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to specifically manipulate proanthocyanidin levels in forage and seed crops for nutritional value and bloat control.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The ast locus appears to be identical to BAN according to the review.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered condensed tannin levels may affect palatability, nutritional quality, and anti-herbivory traits.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Zea mays
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts flavanones such as naringenin and liquiritigenin into isoflavones including genistein and daidzein.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers de novo isoflavone production in transgenic non-legume species.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional screening in yeast/insect cells; transgenic expression in Arabidopsis, tobacco, and maize; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Soybean IFS1 is the first functionally validated isoflavone synthase gene. 2. IFS1 converts naringenin to genistein and, with liquiritigenin available, supports daidzein formation. 3. Transgenic expression in Arabidopsis, tobacco, and maize enables isoflavonoid biosynthesis, especially when combined with chalcone reductase.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A foundational gene for engineering nutritionally valuable isoflavonoids into non-legume crops; should be stacked with chalcone reductase and downstream tailoring enzymes to improve flux and product spectrum.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Naringenin is used less efficiently than liquiritigenin-related substrate routes; co-expression with CHR broadens products to daidzein in maize.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Introduced branch may divert flavanone pools from endogenous flavonoid products.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of isoflavones from flavanone substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Defines the enzymatic entry to isoflavonoid biosynthesis in elicited licorice cells.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene isolation from elicited cell line and biochemical characterization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IFS from Glycyrrhiza echinata confirmed the long-suspected P450 identity of isoflavone synthase. 2. The gene was isolated from an elicited cell line producing isoflavonoids. 3. This discovery established a central tool for engineering isoflavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important donor gene for engineering isoflavonoid production into crops lacking this branch.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates isoflavonoid intermediates at the 2' position in the medicarpin biosynthetic route.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides a required step for medicarpin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA isolation and pathway assignment cited in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. I2'H was isolated from licorice as a cytochrome P450 enzyme. 2. It is another enzyme required for medicarpin biosynthesis. 3. It expands the set of genes needed to engineer complex isoflavonoid phytoalexins.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Needed in multigene stacks aimed at engineering medicarpin-type phytoalexin production.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces vestitone in a late step toward medicarpin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: One of several enzymes required to engineer medicarpin production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic expression attempt referenced in review; prior pathway biochemistry
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VR is identified as one of several enzymes required for medicarpin biosynthesis. 2. Its expression in tobacco was being pursued to engineer phytoalexin production. 3. It represents a necessary downstream tailoring step rather than a stand-alone engineering solution.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in multigene engineering of disease-associated isoflavonoid phytoalexins in heterologous crops.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the B ring to enable synthesis of delphinidin-based purple/blue anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables conversion of flower color toward deep purple when co-expressed with cytochrome b5 in carnation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene isolation; transgenic co-expression in carnation; pigment/color analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Petunia F3'5'H is required for delphinidin-based purple/blue pigment formation. 2. Expression of F3'5'H alone is insufficient for strong color conversion in some ornamentals. 3. Co-introduction with a specific cytochrome b5 transformed carnation flower color from red to deep purple.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential gene for engineering blue/purple flower color, but should be deployed with compatible electron-transfer partners and host metabolic background optimization.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Color outcome depends on host background and cofactor/electron donor compatibility.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: cyanidin-related and other B-ring hydroxylated flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavonoid B ring at the 3' position to alter anthocyanin/flavonoid hydroxylation pattern.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directs flux toward specific pigment classes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls hydroxylation pattern that influences anthocyanin color class and affects localization of flavonoid enzyme complexes in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene isolation by positional cloning/chromosome walking; mutant analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F3'H controls variable B-ring hydroxylation pattern in flavonoids. 2. Petunia and Arabidopsis F3'H genes were isolated, with the Arabidopsis gene corresponding to tt7. 3. In Arabidopsis, deletion of most of the F3'H cytoplasmic domain alters CHS and CHI localization, supporting a membrane-anchor role in enzyme organization.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for modifying pigment hue and potentially pathway organization through branch hydroxylation control.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: multiple tissues including seed coat and roots
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses malonyl-CoA and 4-coumaroyl-CoA to form chalcone.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies core precursor to all downstream flavonoid products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Establishes entry into flavonoid biosynthesis and participates in membrane-associated enzyme complexes affecting flux organization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; protein interaction assays; localization studies; structural analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS catalyzes the first committed step in flavonoid biosynthesis. 2. Arabidopsis CHS physically associates with CHI, F3H, and DFR and localizes to the rough ER. 3. Structural studies provide a basis for engineering catalytic properties and understanding mutation effects.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A central engineering entry point for altering total flavonoid flux, though broad pathway perturbation may cause pleiotropic effects.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation affects multiple flavonoid branches simultaneously.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes stereospecific cyclization of chalcones to flavanones.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for downstream flavonoid products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for early flavonoid formation and participates in flavonoid enzyme complexes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; crystal structure determination; protein interaction and localization studies
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHI is a plant-specific enzyme with unique structure and catalytic activity. 2. Structural work revealed substrate recognition and stereospecific cyclization mechanisms. 3. In Arabidopsis, CHI colocalizes and interacts with CHS in a membrane-associated complex.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to optimize early-pathway efficiency and substrate channeling in engineered flavonoid pathways.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WD40 repeat protein related to heterotrimeric G-protein beta subunits
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive regulation of flavonoid gene expression via regulatory cascade
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes expression of AN2, thereby activating late-pathway anthocyanin genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for proper activation of anthocyanin biosynthesis in petunia.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transposon tagging/positional cloning and expression analysis cited in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AN11 defines a novel class of flavonoid regulatory proteins with WD40 repeats. 2. AN11 is a cytoplasmic protein that regulates expression of the MYB factor AN2. 3. It acts upstream of late anthocyanin gene activation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent upstream regulator of late anthocyanin biosynthesis in petunia flowers.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for coordinated activation of pigment pathways, but likely requires compatible downstream MYB/bHLH networks.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WD40 repeat protein related to heterotrimeric G-protein beta subunits
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: late flavonoid biosynthesis and associated developmental programs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls flavonoid synthesis together with pleiotropic effects on trichomes, root epidermal patterning, and seed mucilage.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis and cloning cited in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTG1 belongs to the WD40 repeat class of flavonoid regulators. 2. TTG1 controls flavonoid synthesis in Arabidopsis. 3. TTG1 also regulates trichome development, root epidermal patterning, and seed mucilage, indicating pleiotropic developmental-metabolic integration.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may alter trichome formation, root epidermal patterning, and seed mucilage in addition to flavonoids.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important pleiotropic regulator of late flavonoid accumulation in Arabidopsis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potentially useful for boosting seed coat flavonoids, but pleiotropic developmental effects require caution.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression causes intensely purple flowers and promotes phenylpropanoid/flavonoid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAP1 is a MYB regulator of phenylpropanoid metabolism in Arabidopsis. 2. Overexpression results in intensely purple flowers. 3. Circadian genomics data implicated PAP1 as a master regulator of coordinated phenylpropanoid pathway expression.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as a master regulator capable of strongly elevating anthocyanin/phenylpropanoid output.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for transcription-factor-based activation of flavonoid accumulation, though broad pathway activation may cause off-target metabolic shifts.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad activation of phenylpropanoid metabolism may alter multiple downstream products.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY family with two SPF1 zinc finger-like domains
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: flavonoid biosynthesis linked to epidermal development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for flavonoid-associated seed coat pigmentation with pleiotropic developmental phenotypes similar to ttg1.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant characterization and cloning cited in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTG2 identifies a novel WRKY class flavonoid regulatory factor. 2. TTG2 has pleiotropic phenotypes similar to ttg1. 3. It supports the idea that overlapping regulatory pathways control flavonoid synthesis and trichome development.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely affects additional developmental traits beyond flavonoid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful for seed coat flavonoid regulation, but developmental pleiotropy limits straightforward deployment.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly activates DFR and a MYB-domain regulator
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates late anthocyanin biosynthesis in petunia.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene identification from regulatory locus and target-gene expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AN1 encodes a novel bHLH transcription factor. 2. AN1 directly activates expression of DFR. 3. AN1 also activates an uncharacterized MYB-domain protein, indicating layered transcriptional regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct activator of key late anthocyanin pathway genes.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for selective activation of late anthocyanin pathway steps in ornamental engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: required for expression of DFR and BAN
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for expression of late flavonoid genes involved in proanthocyanidin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: locus cloning and gene expression requirement analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT8 encodes a bHLH transcription factor involved in late flavonoid regulation. 2. TT8 is required for expression of DFR and BAN. 3. TT8 likely functions analogously to, but is not strictly orthologous with, some known maize/snapdragon regulators.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Critical activator of late genes needed for proanthocyanidin accumulation in Arabidopsis seed coat.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong lever for increasing late flavonoid branch output, especially proanthocyanidins, if combined with compatible partner regulators.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: cells leading to vacuole and cell wall deposition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates 3-deoxy flavonoid synthesis and is associated with fluorescent ER-derived vesicle transport phenomena.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: regulatory analysis and transformed Black Mexican Sweet cell imaging cited in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. P is a maize flavonoid regulator controlling 3-deoxy flavonoid synthesis. 2. P-transformed maize cells revealed ER-derived vesicles containing fluorescent flavonoid compounds. 3. P also exemplifies combinatorial MYB regulation distinct from C1.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for activating phlobaphene/3-deoxyflavonoid traits in cereals for pigmentation and possible defense-related applications.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts with R to regulate flavonoid gene expression in maize.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: protein interaction and regulatory residue analysis cited in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C1 is a classic maize MYB flavonoid regulator. 2. Differential interaction with R is mediated by specific MYB-domain residues. 3. This explains a key feature of combinatorial transcriptional regulation in the maize flavonoid system.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with R in a regulatory complex/module.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for combinatorial activation strategies in maize flavonoid engineering, provided compatible partner bHLH factors are present.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: combinatorial transcriptional activation with MYB partners
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Partner regulator required for combinatorial control of maize flavonoid genes.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. R is a classic maize bHLH regulator of flavonoid biosynthesis. 2. Differential interactions with MYB partners such as C1 and P underlie combinatorial regulation. 3. Specific MYB-domain residues determine partner selectivity.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key cofactor for transcription-factor stacking strategies in maize pigment engineering.
